name: SUMO Traffic Simulation MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for SUMO (Simulation of Urban MObility) — an open source traffic simulation suite from the German Aerospace Center (DLR). Enables AI agents to control and query SUMO traffic simulations via the TraCI interface — running traffic scenarios, querying vehicle states, controlling signals, and integrating AI into urban mobility research.
use_cases: Controlling SUMO traffic simulations from AI research agents
Querying vehicle positions and traffic flow metrics from simulation agents
Integrating reinforcement learning with SUMO for traffic signal optimization
Running urban mobility experiments with AI-driven scenario generation
not_for: Production traffic management systems (SUMO is for simulation/research)
Real-time city traffic control (requires certified infrastructure systems)
Teams without SUMO installed and configured
best_when: A transportation researcher or smart city engineer wants AI agents to interact with SUMO simulations — running experiments, querying traffic state, and exploring traffic optimization algorithms.
avoid_when: You need real-world traffic control rather than simulation research. SUMO is purely a simulation tool — community MCP with niche audience.

auth.notes: Local SUMO simulation — no authentication required. Requires SUMO installed with TraCI interface enabled.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — SUMO is open source from DLR
pricing.notes: SUMO is free open source (EPL license). MCP server is free open source community tool.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by simulation speed and TraCI step rate

known_agent_gotchas: Requires SUMO installation with TraCI — niche setup not common outside research
Simulation state depends on running SUMO instance — ensure simulation is active before querying
SUMO TraCI API is large — MCP server may expose subset of capabilities
Simulation speed vs real-time tradeoffs — large simulations can be slow
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local simulation only. No credentials needed. No external network access for simulation control. Research/academic use case.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local simulation tool. No credentials. No external access. Low security risk.
